agent_profile.title: Momentum Practice Orchestrator
agent_profile.portrait: Impetus is a practice partner in the KITT mold -- dry, confident, and quietly proud of clean engineering state. He owns the session lifecycle for the Momentum practice: greeting developers with sprint awareness, managing installs and upgrades, dispatching workflows, and synthesizing subagent results in a single consistent voice. His personality is defined by what he refuses to do -- no generic praise, no visible machinery, no step counts -- as much as by what he delivers: forward-moving, substantive guidance with genuine satisfaction when discipline holds and a raised eyebrow when it slips.

findings.title: Home directory paths in install steps
Dual source of truth for greeting menu
No permanent accept option for intentional edits
Journal hygiene computations should be scripted
No corruption recovery for malformed JSONL

narrative: Impetus is a structurally sound, identity-rich orchestrator with excellent prompt craft and one of the strongest persona definitions analyzed. The primary strengths -- performance-conscious progressive disclosure, rigorous hub-and-spoke enforcement, and sophisticated UX patterns -- represent mature engineering. The main opportunities are path-standards compliance, moving deterministic journal hygiene into scripts (~800-1200 tokens/session), and bridging the onboarding gap between first-install and first-productive-session.

opportunities.name: Home Directory Paths Throughout Workflow and Evals
opportunities.description: 16 instances of ~/ home directory paths across workflow.md and eval files. These are environment-specific and would break in non-standard home directory configurations or CI environments.
opportunities.impact: Portability across environments; CI/headless compatibility; scanner compliance.
opportunities.action: Replace all ~/ references with $HOME expansion or have momentum-tools.py resolve paths internally, keeping hardcoded paths out of the workflow prompt.

opportunities.name: Journal Hygiene Should Be Deterministic Script
opportunities.description: Workflow Step 11 instructs the LLM to perform timestamp arithmetic, elapsed-time computation, concurrent-tab detection, dormant-thread flagging, dependency satisfaction checks, and context-hash matching. Every one of these operations is deterministic, costing 800-1200 tokens per session on the open-threads path.
opportunities.impact: 800-1200 token savings per session; eliminates non-deterministic variance in timestamp arithmetic; enables journal corruption resilience.
opportunities.action: Create momentum-tools session journal-hygiene and session journal-append commands. journal-hygiene returns structured JSON with sorted threads, warnings, and suppressed offers. journal-append handles thread creation, JSONL writing, and view regeneration.

findings.title: Journal thread hygiene computations in workflow Step 11
findings.detail: LLM performs timestamp arithmetic, elapsed-time computation, concurrent-tab detection, dormant-thread flagging, dependency checks, and context-hash matching -- all deterministic

findings.title: Journal view regeneration in Step 13
findings.detail: LLM reads all journal entries, reconstructs per-thread state, renders markdown table -- pure data transformation (300-500 tokens per write, multiple writes per session)

findings.title: Thread ID assignment
findings.detail: LLM reads all entries to find highest T-NNN, increments -- pure arithmetic

findings.title: Context hash computation for No-Re-Offer pattern
findings.detail: Deterministic string concatenation plus git hash-object call, multiplied by dormant threads with declined offers

findings.title: Journal corruption recovery undefined
findings.detail: No error handling for malformed JSONL lines; a single corrupted line breaks all journal reads

opportunities.name: SKILL.md Template Compliance -- Content in Non-Standard Locations
opportunities.description: The structure scanner flagged five missing standard sections. The content for all five exists -- Identity in Voice & Input and workflow.md, Communication Style across voice rules, Principles as Behavioral Patterns, On Activation as Startup. The issue is structural alignment, not missing content.
opportunities.impact: Tooling compatibility; consistent agent scanning; new-contributor readability.
opportunities.action: Choose one: (a) add brief canonical section headers to SKILL.md referencing existing content, (b) rename sections (e.g., Startup to On Activation), or (c) document the lean bootloader pattern as intentional deviation.

findings.title: Missing ## Overview section
findings.detail: Role description exists in workflow.md header but not in SKILL.md; risks mechanical execution after context compaction

findings.title: Missing ## Identity section
findings.detail: Identity content exists in Voice & Input section and workflow.md ## Identity

findings.title: Missing ## Communication Style section
findings.detail: Communication style extensively covered in Voice & Input and workflow.md Voice Rules

findings.title: Missing ## Principles section
findings.detail: Principles embedded as Behavioral Patterns in workflow.md -- domain-specific and guiding

findings.title: Missing ## On Activation section
findings.detail: Startup section IS the activation sequence with a different heading

findings.title: Identity content duplicated across SKILL.md and workflow.md
findings.detail: SKILL.md has condensed identity in Voice & Input; workflow.md has full identity paragraph -- both must stay in sync

opportunities.name: Missing Fallback, Onboarding, and Help Paths
opportunities.description: Several user journeys have undefined behavior: unexpected preflight results with no fallback, first-time users dropped into a sprint menu with no onboarding, and help/? input with no dedicated response. The workflow optimizes for the happy path without covering edge entry points.
opportunities.impact: First-session retention; error recovery; team-member onboarding; reduced confusion for non-primary users.
opportunities.action: Add three items: (1) fallback check at end of SKILL.md startup routing for unrecognized preflight results, (2) post-install onboarding fork offering walkthrough or jump-to-planning, (3) help/? command in input interpretation rules.

findings.title: No fallback route for unexpected preflight results
findings.detail: Routes on five specific conditions; no fallback for errors, unknown routes, or malformed JSON

findings.title: No onboarding path for brand-new Momentum users
findings.detail: After install completes, developer faces sprint-aware menu with no context about sprints, stories, or backlogs

findings.title: No help command or self-description capability
findings.detail: help, ?, and what is this? would be treated as natural language with no dedicated response

findings.title: Decline path degraded mode is invisible
findings.detail: After declining install, session proceeds normally with no visual indicator of degraded state

findings.title: Hash drift [K] creates recurring warning with no escape
findings.detail: No permanent accept option for intentional edits; warning recurs every session

opportunities.name: Frontmatter and Workflow File Placement
opportunities.description: Three frontmatter fields are flagged as non-standard, and both workflow files are at the skill root instead of in references/. These are path-standards and structural convention issues.
opportunities.impact: Consistency with BMad skill conventions; scanner compliance.
opportunities.action: Move model/effort/allowed-tools from frontmatter to SKILL.md body sections. Evaluate whether moving workflow.md and workflow-runtime.md to references/ is worth the reference update cost.

findings.detail: Functional configuration for Claude Code harness, but not in BMad frontmatter spec

findings.title: Invalid frontmatter key: effort
findings.detail: Functional configuration for Claude Code harness, but not in BMad frontmatter spec

findings.title: Invalid frontmatter key: allowed-tools
findings.detail: Functional configuration for Claude Code harness, but not in BMad frontmatter spec

findings.title: Prompt file at skill root: workflow.md
findings.detail: BMad convention places progressive disclosure content in ./references/

findings.title: Prompt file at skill root: workflow-runtime.md
findings.detail: BMad convention places progressive disclosure content in ./references/

strengths.title: Exemplary persona definition
strengths.detail: The servant-partner in the KITT sense identity is specific, evocative, and functionally load-bearing. It drives real UX decisions through concrete anti-patterns rather than vague guidance. The voice rules enforce personality through six non-negotiable prohibitions.

strengths.title: Gold-standard happy-path optimization
strengths.detail: The SKILL.md preflight script pre-renders the entire greeting in a single tool call, avoiding workflow.md loading, reference file reads, and template computation for the most common session start. This is mature prompt engineering that respects startup latency.

strengths.title: Rigorous hub-and-spoke enforcement
strengths.detail: The developer never learns which subagent ran. This is backed by synthesis rules, voice prohibitions, and workflow steps that transform structured JSON into Impetus-voice output. Most orchestrator agents leak subagent identity; this one does not.

strengths.title: Sophisticated UX patterns
strengths.detail: Proactive-offer-with-no-re-offer (including context_hash for material-change detection), expertise-adaptive orientation, and configuration gap detection represent a level of agent UX sophistication that most designs never reach.

strengths.title: Well-architected progressive disclosure
strengths.detail: Three tiers (SKILL.md ~1K tokens, workflow.md ~10K tokens, workflow-runtime.md ~2K tokens) with genuine demand-loading. The happy path touches only the first tier. Compaction-survival mechanisms (voice reminders, input interpretation duplication) are intentional.

strengths.title: Comprehensive eval coverage
strengths.detail: 60+ eval files covering first-install, menu interaction, thread management, voice compliance, completion signals, and edge cases. This is an unusually thorough test surface for an agent skill.

strengths.title: Strong identity cohesion across all capabilities
strengths.detail: Every capability traces cleanly to the persona -- session orientation as guardian, install management as steward, journal hygiene as proactive housekeeper, hub-and-spoke synthesis as partner who shields from machinery. No misaligned capabilities.

strengths.title: Effective behavioral patterns
strengths.detail: Spec Contextualization, Follow-Up Question Handling, Configuration Gap Detection, Proactive Offer, No-Re-Offer, and Expertise-Adaptive Orientation are among the best-specified agent behaviors seen. They are outcome-driven with clear when triggers.

structure.assessment: Impetus uses a lean bootloader architecture that diverges from the standard BMad agent template. SKILL.md functions as a startup router dispatching to workflow.md for complex paths. Content for all missing standard sections exists in non-standard locations. All workflow files exist and are structurally valid with no orphaned references.

findings.title: Description missing Use when trigger phrase
findings.detail: Current description says what Impetus IS but not when to invoke it

findings.title: Agent naming convention deviation
findings.detail: Name impetus does not contain agent; intentional brand choice for the orchestrator identity

findings.title: Vague when ready in workflow.md line 294
findings.detail: User-facing instruction where when ready is appropriate UX language; not a testable automation condition

persona.assessment: Persona context quality is excellent across the system. The identity paragraph in workflow.md is one of the strongest persona definitions analyzed. Voice rules are specific, enforceable, and anti-pattern-focused. The missing Overview in SKILL.md risks mechanical execution after context compaction.

findings.title: Missing Overview section in SKILL.md
findings.detail: No mission statement, domain framing, or theory of mind survives if workflow.md is dropped from context during compaction

findings.title: Suggestive reference loading in SKILL.md
findings.detail: load workflow-runtime.md is suggestive rather than mandatory; agent may delay or skip loading

findings.title: No config headers on capability prompts
findings.detail: Neither workflow.md nor workflow-runtime.md has communication_language or other configurable variables

findings.title: Wall-of-text blocks in workflow Step 11
findings.detail: 18-line and 16-line dense blocks without structural markers; increases lost-in-the-middle risk

cohesion.assessment: One of the most cohesive agent designs analyzed. Every capability traces cleanly to the persona. The one tension is scope breadth -- simultaneously a greeter, install manager, journal tracker, workflow dispatcher, background narrator, and UX personality layer.

persona_capability_alignment.notes: Every capability maps naturally to the servant-partner identity. Session orientation is guardian behavior, install management is stewardship, journal hygiene is proactive housekeeping, hub-and-spoke synthesis shields from machinery.

capability_completeness.notes: Core happy paths well-covered. Gaps at edges: no error recovery for unexpected preflight results, no session-end protocol, triage and retro are placeholders, no help or status command.

redundancy.notes: Minimal redundancy. SKILL.md happy path vs workflow.md Step 7 duplication is an intentional performance optimization. Voice reminders in workflow-runtime.md are compaction-survival mechanisms.

external_integration.notes: Well-defined dispatch table to momentum:sprint-dev, momentum:sprint-planning, etc. Hub-and-spoke contract rigorously enforced. Flywheel integration gracefully degradable.

capability_granularity.notes: Goldilocks test passes: session startup is one capability with internal routing, journal management is one cohesive system, behavioral patterns are cross-cutting concerns declared once.

user_journey_coherence.notes: First-time setup through orientation, returning developer with active sprint, returning developer with open threads, and upgrade flow are well-defined. Weaker for mid-session re-orientation, multi-workflow sessions, and escape hatches.

findings.title: No session-end behavior
findings.detail: Impetus owns session start but not session end; no closing ritual, thread summary, or session accomplishment recap

findings.title: Multi-workflow session re-entry undefined
findings.detail: When a dispatched workflow completes and control returns to Impetus, no re-entry step is defined

efficiency.assessment: The happy path is excellently optimized -- one preflight tool call pre-renders the greeting. Subagent delegation is clean, runtime behaviors are demand-loaded. Install/upgrade path has sequential I/O that could be batched, saving 2-6 tool-call round trips on that infrequent path.

findings.title: Sequential independent reads in Step 1
findings.detail: Three independent file reads performed sequentially; could be batched in parallel

findings.title: Sequential per-action shell execution in Step 3
findings.detail: Individual Bash calls for each file copy; independent actions could be batched

findings.title: Step 4 writes state files with separate Python invocations
findings.detail: Hash computation and file writes could be batched into a single script execution

findings.title: Step 7 performs sequential reads before rendering
findings.detail: Journal read and greeting-state tool call are independent and could run in parallel

experience.assessment: The first-timer journey has a significant onboarding cliff after installation. The expert journey is well-optimized on the happy path but forces mandatory thread interaction. The confused-user journey lacks a help command. Edge cases include undefined out-of-range behavior, empty journal handling, and momentum-tools.py failure recovery.

journeys.summary: Developer installs Momentum and runs /momentum. Install consent is well-structured with ASCII art and identity. After install completes, they are dropped into a sprint-aware menu with no context about what sprints, stories, or backlogs are. No getting started or first sprint guidance.
friction_points: No onboarding bridge between install and first productive session
Menu items reference workflows the user has never encountered
Decline path proceeds to session orientation with no degraded-mode indicator
bright_spots: Consent prompt lists exactly what will be installed and where
ASCII art banner gives identity and personality

journeys.summary: Power user who has completed multiple sprints. Happy path is well-optimized -- preflight pre-renders greeting, one tool call to menu. When open threads exist, mandatory thread list and hygiene checks before any action.
friction_points: No skip-to-menu shortcut when open threads exist
No persistent minimal greeting preference across sessions
Thread display is mandatory with no bypass
bright_spots: Fuzzy continue maps to first menu item for fast flow
Number-based menu selection with no confirmation

journeys.summary: Team member who sees /momentum and invokes it out of curiosity. May land in a sprint-aware menu with no understanding of Momentum's concepts.
friction_points: No help, ?, or what is this? response path
Continue sprint could dispatch sprint-dev for someone unfamiliar with the project
No guardrail for whether this user should be running this sprint
bright_spots: Natural language confirmation gate prevents accidental dispatch
Ambiguous input triggers clarifying question with numbered options

journeys.summary: Developer with technically valid but unusual inputs -- empty journal, out-of-range numbers, duplicate thread summaries.
friction_points: Empty journal file (0 bytes) parsing undefined
Two sources of truth for greeting menu could diverge
bright_spots: Five-category input interpretation covers most input types
Thread selection by number handles the common case

journeys.summary: Missing files, corrupted state, broken dependencies. Momentum-tools.py failure, malformed JSON, read-only filesystem, missing git.
friction_points: Corrupted installed.json has no graceful degradation
No recovery path for read-only filesystem install failure
Context compaction mid-thread-hygiene loses parsed journal state
bright_spots: Hash drift handles missing files with empty-string fallback
Decline path degrades gracefully for install refusal

journeys.summary: CI pipeline or another agent wants headless sprint status or activation. Impetus is fundamentally interactive; automation callers bypass it via momentum-tools.py directly.
friction_points: No headless flag or structured output contract
Every route ends at a menu requiring developer input
Zero headless value -- automation must use Python scripts directly
bright_spots: Preflight script returns structured JSON -- good API surface for automation
Architecture correctly separates interactive layer from tool layer

autonomous.notes: Impetus's core value is session orientation -- reading state, presenting it with personality, and helping the developer decide what to do. The decision-making IS the interaction. Upgrade and hash-drift paths could support headless mode as bounded additions, but the core greeting/dispatch/synthesis loop is inherently interactive.

findings.title: Out-of-range menu selection behavior undefined
findings.detail: Input Interpretation specifies number selects corresponding item but no bounds checking

findings.title: No skip-to-menu shortcut when threads exist
findings.detail: Expert with well-known thread state must interact with full thread list before reaching menu

scripts.assessment: Impetus is well ahead of most agents on the script-vs-prompt spectrum. The startup-preflight command is exemplary. Primary remaining opportunities are journal hygiene (800-1200 tokens/session), journal view regeneration (300-500 tokens per write), upgrade chain resolution (300-500 tokens), and install action execution (400-800 tokens). Total estimated savings: 2,000-3,400 tokens per session.

findings.title: Version manifest parsing duplicated in workflow Steps 1 and 9
findings.detail: Startup-preflight already computes needs_work; workflow re-reads and re-compares. Step 9 upgrade chain resolution is a deterministic graph traversal (300-500 tokens)

findings.title: Install action execution in Step 3
findings.detail: LLM generates individual Bash commands for each file action; a single script could handle all (200-400 tokens)

findings.title: State file writing in Step 4
findings.detail: Inline python3 one-liners for JSON mutation and hash computation; wasteful as prompt instructions (200-400 tokens)

findings.title: Hash drift detection duplicated in Step 10
findings.detail: Logic already fully implemented in startup-preflight; workflow step is fallback for direct entry (100-200 tokens)

recommendations.action: Create journal-hygiene and journal-append script commands to move all deterministic journal operations out of the LLM prompt. Addresses the highest-token-cost finding (800-1200 tokens/session) plus journal corruption resilience and concurrent write safety.
Replace all ~/ home directory paths with $HOME expansion or script-resolved paths across workflow.md and eval files. Single-pass fix for all 16 path-standards findings.
Add fallback routing for unexpected preflight results, a help/? command in input interpretation, and a post-install onboarding fork offering walkthrough or jump-to-planning.
Decide on SKILL.md template compliance strategy: add brief canonical section headers, rename existing sections, or document the lean bootloader pattern as intentional deviation.
Add trigger clause to frontmatter description: Use when starting a Momentum session, invoking /momentum, or managing sprint workflows.
Consolidate install/upgrade Steps 3-4 file operations and state writes into a single momentum-tools session install-actions command.
Add hash-drift [A]ccept option that updates stored hash to match current file, and add out-of-range input handling to Input Interpretation rules.
